text.title: Perplexity AI Research Workflow Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the perplexity-ai-research methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Compound research questions MUST be decomposed into atomic sub-queries (one fact per query) before hitting the Perplexity API.
rule.rationale: Pro Search distributes its retrieval budget across all parts of a compound query; atomic queries get the full budget.

rule.statement: API calls MUST set search_recency_filter='year' (or tighter) for time-sensitive facts.
rule.rationale: Default broader time windows include stale figures (2-3x off for market data).

rule.statement: Each synthesized claim MUST carry a confidence rating (H/M/L) derived from source count + agreement strength + counter-evidence.

rationale.source: Bayesian qualitative ops 2026; internal pitch-deck audits

rule.rationale: Without per-claim confidence, downstream consumers cannot tell strong evidence from weak.

rule.statement: Perplexity output MUST be reviewed (verified_by) before entering any downstream pipeline.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated citations are the dominant failure mode; verification is the gate.

rule.statement: If question has a single authoritative source OR competitive sensitivity blocks third-party tools, skip.
rule.rationale: Tool overhead is wasted when a direct fetch or 1-1 interview is sharper.
